user_message: Just got this from Sandra Taylor at Northwind Traders -- I need help figuring out QBR preparation. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Northwind Traders)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:08:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:11:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:20:00] Kevin (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-F trap (Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-F failure mode (Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-F -- Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned
